title: Who Goes First
description: Right-of-way at stops, yields, turns, crosswalks, and special situations.

scenarios.prompt: Both cars stop at the same time. Tap the car that goes first.

feedback.correct: Right! When two cars stop at the same time, the car on the right goes first.
feedback.you: When you both stop at the same time, the car on your right goes first. Try again.
feedback.default: When you stop at the same time, the car on the right goes first. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: The other car stopped before you did. Tap the car that goes first.

feedback.correct: Yes! The first car to stop is the first to go.
feedback.you: The other car stopped first, so it goes first. You wait your turn. Try again.
feedback.default: The car that stopped first goes first. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You stopped first. Then a car pulls up on your right. Tap the car that goes first.

feedback.correct: Yes! You stopped first, so you go first — even though the other car is on your right.
feedback.other: You stopped first, so you go first. Getting there first comes before the right-side rule. Try again.
feedback.default: The car that stopped first goes first — that's you. Try again.

scenarios.concept: Four-way stop, you are on the right
scenarios.prompt: You both stop at the same time. The other car is on your left. Tap the car that goes first.

feedback.correct: Yes! The car on the right goes first — and that's you, because the other car is on your left.
feedback.other: When you stop at the same time, the car on the right goes first. You're on the right here. Try again.
feedback.default: The car on the right goes first — that's you. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: Your small road ends at a bigger road. Tap the car that goes first.

feedback.correct: Right! Cars already on the main road go first. You wait, then pull out when it's clear.
feedback.you: Your road ends at the bigger road, so you give way. The car on the main road goes first. Try again.
feedback.default: The car on the main road goes first. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: There are no signs or lights. You both arrive together. Tap the car that goes first.

feedback.correct: Yes! With no signs, you give way to the car on your right.
feedback.you: With no signs, the car on your right goes first. Try again.
feedback.default: Give way to the car on the right. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You have a yield sign. Tap the car that goes first.

feedback.correct: Yes! A yield sign means slow down and let the other car go first, then go when it's clear.
feedback.you: Yield means you wait and let the car already on the road go first. Try again.
feedback.default: Yield means let the other car go first. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You want to turn left. Tap the car that goes first.

feedback.correct: Yes! When you turn left, you let oncoming cars going straight go first.
feedback.you: The car coming straight toward you goes first when you're turning left. Try again.
feedback.default: Let oncoming traffic go first when turning left. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You are entering a roundabout. Tap the car that goes first.

feedback.correct: Yes! Cars already in the roundabout go first. You wait for a gap, then enter.
feedback.you: You yield when entering — cars already going around go first. Try again.
feedback.default: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: Someone is walking in the crosswalk. Tap who goes first.

feedback.correct: Yes! People in the crosswalk always go first. Wait until they finish crossing.
feedback.you: Let the person finish crossing first — pedestrians have the right-of-way. Try again.
feedback.default: The person in the crosswalk goes first. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: An ambulance behind you has its lights and siren on. Tap what you should do.

choices.label: Pull to the right and stop
Speed up to get out of the way
Pull to the left and stop

feedback.correct: Yes! Pull over to the right edge and stop until the emergency vehicle passes.
feedback.fast: Don't speed up — pull to the right and stop so it can pass safely. Try again.
feedback.stay: Move to the right first, then stop — don't block the lane. Try again.
feedback.left: Pull to the right, not the left, so the emergency vehicle can pass. Try again.
feedback.brake: Don't slam your brakes — pull to the right edge and stop smoothly. Try again.
feedback.default: Pull to the right and stop. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: The school bus ahead has red flashing lights and its stop arm out. Tap what you should do.

choices.label: Stop and wait until the lights stop
Pass if you don't see any kids

feedback.correct: Yes! Red flashing lights mean stop and wait until they turn off and the stop arm folds in.
feedback.pass: Never pass a stopped school bus with red lights flashing — children may be crossing. Stop and wait. Try again.
feedback.honk: Stop and wait quietly until the red lights turn off. Try again.
feedback.slow: Slowing down isn't enough — you must fully stop and wait. Try again.
feedback.nokids: Stop even if you don't see kids — they may be about to cross. Wait for the lights to turn off. Try again.
feedback.default: Stop and wait for the red lights to turn off. Try again.
